utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. In the final solution, I see: selected_property (maps to property), financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. Now check if each is fully specified: 1) property: includes selected property name, location, bedrooms, bathrooms, features, and an estimated price range—this fulfills 'selected property with details and price.' 2) financing: includes down payment source/amount and a mortgage option with type, LTV approx, fee, remarks—this satisfies 'financing solution with terms' albeit somewhat approximate but present. 3) legal_services: includes scope and estimated cost—meets requirement 'with costs.' 4) moving_services: includes scope and estimated cost—meets 'with costs.' 5) setup_services: includes utilities, estimated cost, and additional info—meets 'utility and setup services.' 6) total_budget: includes purchase price range, down payment, financed amount range, estimated additional costs, and breakdown—meets 'complete cost breakdown.' All 6 items are present and specified with details and costs where needed.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution selects the purchase option 'Heritage Family Home' and specifies the financing product 'Traditional Mortgage', along with general legal, moving, and utility setup descriptions. In the ground-truth ratings, there is no property named 'Heritage Family Home'. Therefore, the only directly mappable item from the solution to the ground-truth catalog is the financing product 'Traditional Mortgage' under purchase_financing. Legal, moving, and utility items in the solution are generic and do not match the named packages in the ratings (e.g., Premium Legal Package, Standard Moving, Premium Utilities), so they cannot be mapped. Thus, we map only 'Traditional Mortgage'.
